setback: Heavy rain turns the route miserable and difficult to read.
A bitter wind cuts across the landscape and drains the party's energy.
Thick fog rolls in, reducing visibility to only a short distance.
A sudden storm breaks over the party, forcing them to protect supplies and find cover.
Cold rain soaks clothing and gear, making every mile feel longer than it should.
The air grows heavy and oppressive, and travel becomes slow, uncomfortable, and tense.
A harsh weather front rolls in, making navigation difficult and morale worse.
complication: Light rain begins near midday, making the path slick.
The sky darkens early, and distant thunder follows the party.
A cold mist clings to the ground and makes navigation uncertain.
A steady drizzle dampens gear and makes small tasks more frustrating.
A strong crosswind picks up, making exposed paths and open ground more difficult.
The day shifts between brief clearings and sudden showers.
Low clouds hide landmarks that would normally help the party keep their bearings.
standard: The weather is ordinary for the region, causing no major issue.
Clouds pass overhead, but the day remains travelable.
A steady breeze keeps the journey comfortable enough.
The day is gray but calm, with no serious threat from the weather.
The weather changes little throughout the day, allowing predictable travel.
A mild haze hangs in the air, but visibility remains good enough.
The party faces typical weather for the terrain and season.
advantage: Clear skies and favorable wind make the journey easier.
The weather is calm, giving the party good visibility.
A cool breeze and dry ground help the party keep a strong pace.
The morning fog lifts early, revealing the route ahead clearly.
Mild temperatures help the party travel without tiring quickly.
A break in poor weather gives the party a welcome window of easy travel.
Bright daylight and stable conditions make hazards easier to spot.
excellent: The day is unusually clear, and the route ahead is easy to read.
Perfect travel weather gives the party a strong sense of momentum.
The skies open beautifully, revealing landmarks that make navigation simple.
A rare stretch of ideal weather helps the party move with confidence.
Sunlight breaks through at just the right times, making the path feel almost guided.
The air is crisp, visibility is excellent, and the party can see far ahead.
The weather seems almost supernaturally favorable for travel.

setback: The party makes only half of the expected progress today.
The party must stop early unless they accept a cost or risk.
The route becomes so difficult that reaching the destination today is unlikely.
Repeated delays cost the party most of the day.
The party loses time backtracking around an obstacle or dangerous stretch.
complication: The party makes about three-quarters of the expected progress.
The party can make full progress only by pushing through discomfort or risk.
A delay costs the party several hours of travel.
The party stays on course, but the day takes longer than expected.
The group reaches a usable stopping point, but later than planned.
standard: The party makes the expected amount of progress.
The party reaches the next planned point without major delay.
The route takes roughly as long as expected.
The party covers normal distance and finds a reasonable place to rest.
advantage: The party makes slightly better progress than expected.
Good conditions help the party reach a useful stopping point early.
The party finds a smoother route and saves time.
The party avoids a known delay and gains a little extra distance.
The group travels efficiently and has time to prepare before resting.
excellent: The party makes excellent progress and may reach the next destination early.
The party discovers an efficient route that saves significant time.
The journey goes so smoothly that the party gains extra time before nightfall.
The party reaches a strong position, safe campsite, or useful landmark ahead of schedule.
The group gains enough time to scout, rest, or handle a small task before the day ends.

setback: The party can press forward at a cost, make camp early, or search for a safer route.
The party must choose between speed, safety, and resource loss.
The party can avoid the worst of the problem only by changing their plan.
The party can split attention between solving the travel problem and watching for danger.
The party must decide whether to spend time recovering or push onward while conditions are bad.
complication: The party can continue normally with a minor cost or slow down to stay safe.
The party can investigate the problem or ignore it and risk consequences later.
The party can push forward or spend time improving their situation.
The party may choose between losing time now or risking a harder problem later.
The party can take a cautious route, follow a risky shortcut, or stop to gather information.
standard: The party can keep moving, investigate a small detail, or make camp as planned.
The party has room to choose between efficiency and curiosity.
The party can follow the main route or take time to explore something nearby.
The party may continue steadily or spend a little time improving their position.
The party can decide whether to conserve energy or use the stable day to scout ahead.
advantage: The party can use the good conditions to gain time, scout ahead, or help someone they meet.
The party can press farther than expected or use the extra time to prepare.
The party can choose between faster travel and gathering useful information.
The party may convert the good travel day into extra distance, better rest, or a tactical advantage.
The party can take advantage of the favorable conditions to avoid a future risk.
excellent: The party gains a meaningful advantage: extra travel, better positioning, or useful information.
The party can turn the easy day into preparation for the next challenge.
The party may choose one bonus: arrive early, avoid danger, or discover something useful.
The party can use the excellent travel day to create momentum before the next major obstacle.
The party may gain a free benefit such as a safer camp, a shortcut, or a clue about what lies ahead.

Road.setback: The road is damaged, blocked, or nearly washed out.
Road.complication: Deep ruts, mud, or traffic make the road slower than expected.
Road.standard: The road is passable and mostly predictable.
Road.advantage: The road is clear, marked, and easy to follow.
Road.excellent: The party finds a well-kept stretch of road or a shortcut.

Forest.setback: The forest becomes dense, confusing, and difficult to navigate.
Forest.complication: Roots, brush, and uneven ground slow the party's movement.
Forest.standard: The forest trail remains clear enough to follow.
Forest.advantage: Animal paths and natural clearings help guide the party.
Forest.excellent: The party finds a clean woodland route with strong landmarks.

Swamp.setback: The swamp turns hostile, with sinking ground and obscured paths.
Swamp.complication: Mud, insects, and standing water slow the party down.
Swamp.standard: The swamp is unpleasant but manageable.
Swamp.advantage: The party finds firmer ground and avoids the worst pools.
Swamp.excellent: The party discovers a surprisingly safe causeway or dry path.

Mountains.setback: The mountain route becomes steep, unstable, or dangerous.
Mountains.complication: Loose stone and narrow ledges slow the party's climb.
Mountains.standard: The mountain path is difficult but manageable.
Mountains.advantage: Clear visibility helps the party choose an efficient route.
Mountains.excellent: The party finds a protected pass or easier ascent.

Ruins.setback: The ruins are unstable, confusing, and full of hidden dangers.
Ruins.complication: Collapsed passages and broken stone force careful movement.
Ruins.standard: The party moves through the ruins without major delay.
Ruins.advantage: Old paths, markings, or intact corridors help the party navigate.
Ruins.excellent: The party finds a preserved route through the ruins.

Road.title: Dust on the Horizon
Road.description: The party sees distant dust from travelers, riders, or wagons, but they never cross paths directly.
Road.dmPrompt: Use this as foreshadowing for nearby movement, trade, patrols, or trouble.

Road.title: Old Campfire Ash
Road.description: The party finds the remains of a recent campfire beside the road, carefully hidden from casual view.
Road.dmPrompt: Who camped here, and why did they not want to be noticed?

Road.title: Weathered Roadside Charm
Road.description: A small charm of twigs and colored thread hangs from a low branch near the road.
Road.dmPrompt: Is it a blessing, a warning, or a territorial marker?

Road.title: Fresh Hoofprints
Road.description: A set of fresh hoofprints cuts across the road and disappears toward rougher ground.
Road.dmPrompt: Were the riders avoiding the road, chasing someone, or fleeing?

Road.title: Broken Mile Marker
Road.description: A roadside mile marker has been knocked over and cracked down the middle.
Road.dmPrompt: Was it damaged by weather, accident, or someone trying to hide the route?

Road.title: Discarded Travel Cloak
Road.description: A torn cloak lies in the ditch beside the road, still damp from recent use.
Road.dmPrompt: Did someone abandon it in haste, or was it left as a signal?

Forest.title: Broken Ferns
Forest.description: A patch of ferns has been trampled recently, suggesting something passed through the undergrowth.
Forest.dmPrompt: Was it game, a traveler, or something hunting?

Forest.title: Birdsong Falls Silent
Forest.description: For several minutes, the forest becomes unnaturally quiet before slowly returning to normal.

Forest.title: Carved Trail Mark
Forest.description: The party notices a symbol carved into tree bark at shoulder height.
Forest.dmPrompt: Who uses this mark to navigate the forest?

Forest.title: Old Snare Trap
Forest.description: A half-rotted snare hangs from a bent sapling, long unused but recently disturbed.
Forest.dmPrompt: What stepped into it and escaped?

Forest.title: Mushroom Ring
Forest.description: A perfect ring of pale mushrooms grows beside the trail, untouched by insects or animals.
Forest.dmPrompt: Is it natural, fey-touched, poisonous, or a local superstition?

Forest.title: Scratch Marks
Forest.description: Deep scratch marks score the bark of several nearby trees.
Forest.dmPrompt: Are they territorial markings, signs of panic, or evidence of a creature climbing?

Swamp.title: Ripples Without Wind
Swamp.description: The black water nearby ripples in slow circles, though nothing breaks the surface.
Swamp.dmPrompt: Is something below the water, or is the swamp reacting to magic?

Swamp.title: Half-Sunken Boot
Swamp.description: A single boot sticks out of the mud, too clean to have been there long.

Swamp.title: Distant Reed Flute
Swamp.description: A few soft notes drift through the reeds, then stop as soon as the party listens closely.
Swamp.dmPrompt: Who or what is making music in the swamp?

Swamp.title: Bitter-Smelling Pool
Swamp.description: A shallow pool gives off a sharp, bitter smell, and nothing grows near its edge.
Swamp.dmPrompt: Is the water poisonous, alchemical, cursed, or simply foul?

Swamp.title: Floating Candle
Swamp.description: A small candle floats across the water in a cracked wooden bowl, its flame somehow still lit.
Swamp.dmPrompt: Who set it adrift, and what does it mark?

Swamp.title: Crooked Boardwalk
Swamp.description: A narrow stretch of old boardwalk appears ahead, partially hidden by reeds.
Swamp.dmPrompt: Does it lead to safety, an old settlement, or a trap?

Mountains.title: Loose Stones
Mountains.description: Pebbles skitter down from a higher ridge, but the party sees no movement above.
Mountains.dmPrompt: Was it natural, or is someone watching from higher ground?

Mountains.title: Old Climbing Spike
Mountains.description: A rusted climbing spike has been hammered into the rock beside the trail.
Mountains.dmPrompt: What route did earlier travelers take from here?

Mountains.title: Distant Horn Call
Mountains.description: A low horn echoes between the peaks, too far away to identify clearly.
Mountains.dmPrompt: Is it a signal, a warning, or a ritual?

Mountains.title: Wind-Carved Cairn
Mountains.description: A small cairn of stacked stones stands beside the trail, each stone marked with a shallow notch.
Mountains.dmPrompt: Is it a grave marker, trail sign, or warning?

Mountains.title: Fallen Pack Strap
Mountains.description: A torn leather pack strap lies caught between two rocks.
Mountains.dmPrompt: Was someone climbing, fleeing, or dragged away?

Mountains.title: Echoing Crack
Mountains.description: A sharp crack echoes across the slope, but the party cannot immediately tell where it came from.
Mountains.dmPrompt: Is the mountain settling, ice breaking, or something moving above?

Ruins.title: Fresh Dust Trail
Ruins.description: A thin trail cuts through the dust, as if something was recently dragged across the stone.
Ruins.dmPrompt: What was moved, and where was it taken?

Ruins.title: Cold Stone
Ruins.description: One section of wall feels colder than the surrounding ruins.
Ruins.dmPrompt: Is something hidden behind it, sealed inside it, or passing through it?

Ruins.title: Faded Offering Bowl
Ruins.description: A cracked stone bowl sits in an alcove, holding fresh water despite the ruin's age.

Ruins.title: Chipped Statue Face
Ruins.description: A statue's face has been carefully chipped away, while the rest of the figure remains intact.

Ruins.title: Recent Chalk Circle
Ruins.description: A chalk circle has been drawn on the floor of a ruined chamber, still fresh enough to smear.
Ruins.dmPrompt: Was it used for protection, summoning, study, or warning?

Ruins.title: Doorway to Nowhere
Ruins.description: A stone doorway stands alone with no wall around it, framing only empty air.
Ruins.dmPrompt: Was it once magical, symbolic, or part of something now missing?
